rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MILES VS. MARC SHISHIDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH MILES' Motion To Enforce Settlement DENIED. This action has been dismissed without obtaining a Court order retaining jurisdiction. It is not enough for parties to stipulate under CCP 664.6, the Court must retain jurisdiction. There is no evidence provided by the moving party that that parties requested that the Court retain jurisdiction and that the Court did in fact retain jurisdiction. Additionally, even if the Court had jurisdiction the motion would be DENIED. The settlement agreement is not specific enough for enforcement. At the time the settlement agreement was entered into the parties were on notice that they had a difference of opinions as the number of wooden boxes in Defendant's possession - Plaintiff claimed that as of 2009 (the date of last invoice) he had 159 boxes and Defendant estimated that there were 50 boxes in the garage. The parties failed to resolve this inconsistency and entered into the settlement agreement whereas Defendants were to turn over boxes in their possession. Settlement does not provide a number and the Plaintiff fails to provide competent admissible evidence as to the number of boxes in the garage in 2011 (at the time Plaintiff lost possession). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID ABRAMOVITZ ET AL VS. FARHAT BAJJALIYA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-557141 WITH CASE CGC-16-550493

Actions are consolidated for trial purposes only. Moving party to prepare order. = (501/REQ)
